async def test_generate_pin_service_default_length(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    mock_lock_config_entry,
    lock_code_manager_config_entry,
) -> None:
    """generate_pin returns a 4-digit PIN by default."""
    response = await hass.services.async_call(
        DOMAIN,
        SERVICE_GENERATE_PIN,
        {},
        blocking=True,
        return_response=True,
    )
    assert response is not None
    assert "pin" in response
    pin = response["pin"]
    assert len(pin) == 4
    assert pin.isdigit()
    assert not is_unsafe_pin(pin)


async def test_generate_pin_service_custom_length(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    mock_lock_config_entry,
    lock_code_manager_config_entry,
) -> None:
    """generate_pin honours the length field."""
    response = await hass.services.async_call(
        DOMAIN,
        SERVICE_GENERATE_PIN,
        {ATTR_LENGTH: 6},
        blocking=True,

async def test_deobfuscate_log_service_round_trips_configured_pins(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    mock_lock_config_entry,
    lock_code_manager_config_entry,
) -> None:
    """deobfuscate_log replaces tokens for currently-configured PINs and wraps with sentinels."""
    instance_id = hass.data[DOMAIN]["instance_id"]
    # BASE_CONFIG has slot 1 with PIN "1234" and slot 2 with PIN "5678".
    token_slot_1 = mask_pin("1234", 1, instance_id)
    token_slot_2 = mask_pin("5678", 2, instance_id)
    log_excerpt = (
        f"Setting usercode on lock.test_1 slot 1 (pin={token_slot_1})\n"
        f"Setting usercode on lock.test_2 slot 2 (pin={token_slot_2})\n"
        "Unrelated line with no token\n"
        "Stale token from a rotated PIN: pin#deadbeef"
    )

    response = await hass.services.async_call(
        DOMAIN,
        SERVICE_DEOBFUSCATE_LOG,
        {ATTR_TEXT: log_excerpt},
        blocking=True,
        return_response=True,
    )

    assert response is not None
    text = response["deobfuscated_text"]
    assert "1234" in text
    assert "5678" in text
    assert token_slot_1 not in text
    assert token_slot_2 not in text
    # Stale token is preserved verbatim so the output remains comparable to the input.
    assert "pin#deadbeef" in text
    assert "BEGIN DEOBFUSCATED" in text and "END DEOBFUSCATED" in text

    summary = response["summary"]
    assert summary == {
        "total": 3,
        "matched": 2,
        "unmatched_tokens": ["pin#deadbeef"],
    }

1	"""Utility functions for lock_code_manager."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from collections.abc import Iterable
6	import logging
7	import re
8	from typing import Any
9	import zlib
10	
11	from homeassistant.components.switch import DOMAIN as SWITCH_DOMAIN, SERVICE_TURN_OFF
12	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
13	from homeassistant.const import ATTR_ENTITY_ID, CONF_ENABLED, CONF_PIN
14	from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant
15	from homeassistant.helpers import entity_registry as er
16	from homeassistant.helpers.issue_registry import IssueSeverity, async_create_issue
17	
18	from ..const import DOMAIN
19	from .config import EntryConfig, build_slot_unique_id
20	
21	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	def mask_pin(
25	    pin: str | None,
26	    slot_num: int | str,
27	    instance_id: str,
28	) -> str:
29	    """
30	    Return a deterministic masked representation of a PIN for logging.
31	
32	    Uses CRC32 salted with the HA instance ID and slot number so the same
33	    PIN on the same slot always produces the same 8-char hex token regardless
34	    of which lock or config entry it belongs to.  Different slots or HA
35	    instances produce different tokens.
36	    """
37	    if not pin:
38	        return "<empty>"
39	    salt = f"{instance_id}:{slot_num}:{pin}"
40	    return f"pin#{zlib.crc32(salt.encode()) & 0xFFFFFFFF:08x}"
41	
42	
43	# Token format emitted by ``mask_pin``: literal "pin#" followed by 8 lowercase
44	# hex chars. Anchored by word boundary on the right so a longer hex string
45	# isn't truncated and reported as a match.
46	_PIN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"pin#[0-9a-f]{8}\b")
47	
48	
49	def build_pin_deobfuscation_map(
50	    entries: Iterable[ConfigEntry], instance_id: str
51	) -> dict[str, str]:
52	    """
53	    Build a ``{masked_token: plaintext_pin}`` lookup for every configured PIN.
54	
55	    Reads each entry's options-or-data view via ``EntryConfig`` so it works
56	    whether or not the entry has been migrated to options yet. Slots whose
57	    PIN is empty are skipped because ``mask_pin`` returns ``<empty>`` for
58	    them and that's not a token we need to reverse.
59	
60	    Different ``(slot, pin)`` pairs always produce different tokens (slot
61	    is part of the salt), so the resulting map has one entry per
62	    configured slot with a PIN.
63	    """
64	    table: dict[str, str] = {}
65	    for entry in entries:
66	        config = EntryConfig.from_entry(entry)
67	        for slot_num, slot_config in config.slots.items():
68	            pin = slot_config.get(CONF_PIN)
69	            if not pin:
70	                continue
71	            table[mask_pin(pin, slot_num, instance_id)] = pin
72	    return table
73	
74	
75	def deobfuscate_pins(text: str, table: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
76	    """
77	    Replace ``pin#xxxxxxxx`` tokens in ``text`` using ``table``.
78	
79	    Tokens not in the table are left verbatim so the output stays
80	    paste-compatible with the original log (useful when only some PINs
81	    have been rotated since the log was written). The summary lists the
82	    distinct unmatched tokens so the caller can see what didn't resolve
83	    without scanning the text.
84	    """
85	    counts = {"total": 0, "matched": 0}
86	    unmatched: set[str] = set()
87	
88	    def _replace(match: re.Match[str]) -> str:
89	        token = match.group(0)
90	        counts["total"] += 1
91	        if token in table:
92	            counts["matched"] += 1
93	            return table[token]
94	        unmatched.add(token)
95	        return token
96	
97	    deobfuscated = _PIN_TOKEN_RE.sub(_replace, text)
98	    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
99	        "total": counts["total"],
100	        "matched": counts["matched"],
101	        "unmatched_tokens": sorted(unmatched),
102	    }
103	    return deobfuscated, summary
104	
105	
106	async def async_disable_slot(
107	    hass: HomeAssistant,
108	    ent_reg: er.EntityRegistry,
109	    config_entry_id: str,
110	    slot_num: int,
111	    *,
112	    reason: str | None = None,
113	    lock_name: str | None = None,
114	    lock_entity_id: str | None = None,
115	) -> bool:
116	    """
117	    Disable a slot via the enabled switch and optionally create a repair issue.
118	
119	    Returns True if the switch was found and turned off, False otherwise.
120	    When reason is provided, a repair issue is created so the user can
121	    acknowledge it through the Home Assistant repairs dashboard.
122	    """
123	    enabled_entity_id = ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
124	        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
125	        DOMAIN,
126	        build_slot_unique_id(config_entry_id, slot_num, CONF_ENABLED),
127	    )
128	    if not enabled_entity_id:
129	        lock_context = f" on {lock_name} ({lock_entity_id})" if lock_name else ""
130	        _LOGGER.warning(
131	            "Cannot disable slot %s%s — switch entity not found",
132	            slot_num,
133	            lock_context,
134	        )
135	        return False
136	
137	    await hass.services.async_call(
138	        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
139	        SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
140	        {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_entity_id},
141	        blocking=True,
142	    )
143	
144	    if reason:
145	        issue_id = f"slot_disabled_{config_entry_id}_{slot_num}"
146	        async_create_issue(
147	            hass,
148	            DOMAIN,
149	            issue_id,
150	            is_fixable=True,
151	            is_persistent=True,
152	            severity=IssueSeverity.WARNING,
153	            translation_key="slot_disabled",
154	            translation_placeholders={
155	                "slot_num": str(slot_num),
156	                "reason": reason,
157	            },
158	        )
159	
160	    return True

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add deobfuscate_log service to reverse masked PIN tokens in logs

mask_pin() emits "pin#xxxxxxxx" tokens in log lines so users can share
logs without leaking PINs. The reverse direction is also useful for
local debugging — a user pasting their own logs to see which slot a
warning refers to. Build the inverse: enumerate every (slot, pin) pair
across all loaded LCM entries, precompute mask_pin for each, and
substitute matches in pasted text.

New service ``lock_code_manager.deobfuscate_log`` (response-only):
- Input: ``text`` — pasted log content
- Output:
  - ``deobfuscated_text``: input with tokens replaced, wrapped in
    "=== BEGIN DEOBFUSCATED — DO NOT SHARE ===" sentinel so users
    notice the response contains plaintext PINs before pasting it
    anywhere shared
  - ``summary``: ``{total, matched, unmatched_tokens}``

Unmatched tokens (PINs rotated since the log was written, or from
other instances) are preserved verbatim so the output stays
paste-compatible with the original log. They are listed in the
summary so the caller can see what didn't resolve.

The masking uses CRC32 (not cryptographic), so this service does not
change the threat model — a motivated attacker who knows the salt
format can already brute-force 4-12 digit PINs against the tokens in
milliseconds. The service is for the legitimate user's own debugging.

Tests cover the lookup builder, token substitution (matched/unmatched/
mixed/repeated/partial tokens), summary correctness, and the
end-to-end service round trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
